query_normalized: the impact of perspective-taking on moral outrage in online discourse psychology
rationale: The paper investigates the moral reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) and their alignment with human responses, rather than studying the psychological mechanism of "perspective-taking" or its causal impact on "moral outrage" in human online discourse. While it touches on moral psychology, it fails to address the specific independent variable (perspective-taking), the specific dependent variable (moral outrage), or the specific domain (human online discourse) required by the use
